# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent kong no auth.
# Category: kong_no_auth

services:
  - name: service-a
    url: http://service-a:8080
    routes:
      - name: route-a
        paths:
          - /service-a
        methods:
          - GET
          - POST
    plugins:
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key_names:
            - api_key

  - name: service-b
    url: http://service-b:8080
    routes:
      - name: route-b
        paths:
          - /service-b
        methods:
          - GET
          - PUT
    plugins:
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key_names:
            - api_key

upstreams:
  - name: upstream-service-a
    targets:
      - target: service-a:8080
        weight: 100
      - target: service-a-backup:8080
        weight: 50

  - name: upstream-service-b
    targets:
      - target: service-b:8080
        weight: 100
      - target: service-b-backup:8080
        weight: 50

plugins:
  - name: rate-limiting
    config:
      second: 5
      hour: 1000
      policy: local
      limit_by: consumer

  - name: cors
    config:
      origins: 
        - "*"
      methods: 
        - GET
        - POST
        - PUT
      headers: 
        - "Authorization"
        - "Content-Type"
      expose_headers: 
        - "X-RateLimit-Limit"
        - "X-RateLimit-Remaining"
      max_age: 3600
      credentials: true
